title: Written ID – English
summary: Everything we say, write, and do is guided by our purpose:

columns.caption: We exist to positively shape the future  with responsible advice and capital. Today and for generations to come.

columns.title: Our starting point
columns.content: At SEB, we have a long history of helping our customers. Through good times and bad, we’ve stood by their side with responsible advice and capital.Our customers perceive us as experts. They bring us their concerns, questions, and dreams about the future.But our expertise means nothing if we fail to convey it. Which is why we always keep the customer in mind when interacting with them. We dare to be human and relate to them on a personal level.

columns.title: Why do we need a Written Identity?
columns.content: At SEB we are thousands of people in many markets and offices around the globe. A clear written identity helps us maintain a distinct voice and unified experience.Our Written Identity isn’t just about rules and grammar, do’s and don’ts. It's about how we express ourselves and the feeling we want to leave people with. Of course we must write in a professional way, but the most important thing is communicating a clear message in a collective SEB voice.

sections.title: This is how we express ourselves

columns.title: 01. One voice, different levels
columns.content:
As a bank, we talk to many different people in many different contexts. Often it’s existing or prospective customers, but it can also be decision-makers, politicians, or journalists.What we say – and how we say it – must be adapted to the target group. Are we teaching kids about money in an app? 

Sending a letter home to young customers on the cusp of adulthood? Inviting an existing customer to an exclusive event?Our message is shaped by the overall intention, the media channel we’re using, and the target group’s level of knowledge. In many cases, the text is combined with visual material to strengthen our message.



### Example – speaking to children, welcome to adulthood!

Helping young children understand the value of money is important and requires us to simplify our communication. Instead of using new and difficult terms like “balance” or “available amount” we can say, for example, “money on the card”.  

When existing customers turn 18, they’re able to take more financial responsibility. They also gain access to a wider range of SEB services. To emphasize this, we send a letter home encouraging them to get started with their new banking opportunities.  

Instead of the formal “To you who are coming of age” we express ourselves more enthusiastically with “So let’s get started!”.

columns.title: 02. Simplify, simplify, simplify
columns.content:
We use a relaxed and human tone, yet we avoid being too youthful or laid back. We maintain the seriousness expected of a bank without communicating in an overly formal, making every word count, we respect people’s time. Simple and clear words are preferable to longer, more complex ones. If we need to use complicated terms or expressions, we explain them and make them as easy to understand as possible.We also try to avoid internal abbreviations, which can lead to miscommunication and possibly exclude people. Our language is inclusive and never reinforces negative stereotypes.

### Example – Reducing complexity and one language for all

Finance is a complicated topic for many. By using clear and inspiring language, we can lower the barriers and encourage more people to take responsibility for their financial future.  

Instead of writing, ”Investing in funds can be beneficial because you get a return on your investments if the fund goes up in value.” we write “Invest in mutual funds and give your money a chance to grow.”

Inclusive writing is largely about avoiding ingrained stereotypes and overly casual expressions.

In an app experience for kids, we can’t assume that everyone has a parent. Therefore, we use a more inclusive word such as “adult”. We also avoid exclusionary expressions. Instead of “girls and guys” we say “everyone”.

columns.title: 03. Confident and inclusive
columns.content:
A  confident tone assures our customers that we can take care of them. That said, our attitude remains open and humble. Though we’re a large and serious bank, we communicate on a personal level – from person to person.Part of our job is understanding each customer’s unique situation, not just offering one and the same solution for all. From that understanding, we can offer better advice and be of service to people in any situation.We embody our long legacy without becoming too nostalgic. Our experience gives us a deep level of knowledge, but we’re always open to learning new things and seeing in new ways.

### Example – Your local experts and a unique solution for you

As a leading corporate bank, we want to show large companies in the Nordics that we can be their local Cash Management bank. Both globally and with experts in each local market.

With clear and convincing facts, we highlight the breadth of our expertise: “167 years. 50 currencies. 26 locations.”

Talking about interest rates can be difficult when we don’t offer the lowest market price or the same rate to everyone. What we can offer is genuine service and an interest rate that feels tailored to the individual.   

Instead of “Right now! 4.24% mortgage rate” we say “We take your interest rate personally. See what mortgage rate we can offer you.”

columns.title: 04. Professional problem-solvers
columns.content:
Each interaction should have a clear purpose with well-defined next steps. Our customers should know exactly what’s expected of them after receiving the message.We offer concrete advice–both for today and the future–and help customers take responsibility for their financial health. As often as possible, we guide them in solving problems on their own while assuring them we’re available if needed.We encourage customers to reach out to us and do whatever we can to lower any barriers. In relevant situations, we use knowledgeable experts and spokespeople to underline our expertise.

### Example – Help whenever you need it and a bank for entrepreneurs

The fact that we’ve stopped drop-ins at retail locations may arouse emotions, but it actually improves our ability to prepare for a customer’s case and provide better service. Our communication must help customers see the benefits.  

We write on our retail location doors: “Book your visit in advance so can offer you the best service. Our call center is always open if you need help now.”

We’ve supported many entrepreneurs and want to show that we’re there for them every step of the way. From idea to reality.  

Instead of saying “We’re a knowledgeable corporate bank”, we keep the entrepreneur in focus and say “Good ideas need a good bank”.

columns.title: Checklist
columns.content:
You’re an expert in your field, but here is a general checklist to use in your communication. Answer these questions to see if you’re expressing a tonality that’s true to SEB.

- Does the message convey expertise and understanding of the customer’s challenges.
- Does the communication feel person-to-person?
- Is it adapted to the target group and the chosen channel?
- Are there any unnecessary words that can be removed, or any long and difficult words that can be re-worded to make the text easier to understand?
- Are there any expressions that could be interpreted as inappropriate or exclusionary?
- Will the recipient know what's expected of them after reading the message?
- Do the text and visuals complement each other well?

columns.title: Going deeper
columns.content:
If you have specific language questions or need guidance on certain topics, there’s more material to explore on the intranet and in Brand Hub.

- Guidelines for grammar, internal expressions, abbreviations, and specific situations are gathered on [a platform here](https://sebgroup.sharepoint.com/sites/CXCentral/SitePages/Writing.aspx).
- On the intranet you’ll also find examples of how to write for social media, UX text, internal communication, and the like. [Communication around sustainability in funds has its own guidelines.](https://brand.seb.se/point/en/seb/component/private/110751)
- There are also updated guidelines for [digital invitations](https://brand.seb.se/point/en/seb/component/private/110841).

columns.title: Digital invitations
columns.excerpt: Be concise and clear. What is the purpose of the invitation and who is it for Avoid lengthy intros, complex jargon, and wordy sentences.

columns.title: Talking about sustainability
columns.excerpt: Guidelines for sustainability terminology in fund information. At SEB, we strive to always express ourselves clearly and transparently to ensure that customers receive correct information and understand the choices they make.
